enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each record's parsed content; the set of meta-information lines is identical, only their order differs

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

preconditions: header contains at least one structured ##INFO, ##FORMAT, or ##FILTER line with multiple key=value pairs
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — reordering key=value pairs inside structured meta-lines does not change the parsed header dictionary

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent remapping of Number=A, Number=R, and GT fields
enforced.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and consistently remapping GT indices and Number=A/Number=R INFO/FORMAT values, the parsed variant semantics must be identical: all allele-dependent fields (GT, Number=A values, Number=R values) must reflect the new ordering such that the biological meaning is preserved. The REF allele (index 0) must never change.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
G: The field has one value for each possible genotype. The values must be in the same order as prescribed in section <ref> (see Genotype Ordering).

enforced.mr_name: Reject VCF with ##fileformat not on first line
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to parse) a file where the ##fileformat line is not the first line. Silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Reject GT allele index exceeding number of ALT alleles
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a record where a GT allele index references an ALT allele that does not exist (index > len(ALT)). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: Reject INFO Number=A field with wrong cardinality (extra value)
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a record where an INFO field declared Number=A has a number of values that does not equal the number of ALT alleles. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

The Number entry is an Integer that describes the number of values that can be included with the INFO field. For example, if the INFO field contains a single number, then this value must be 1; if the INFO field describes a pair of numbers, then this value must be 2 and so on.

enforced.mr_name: POS and REF first-base relationship invariance under BCF round-trip
enforced.oracle: After VCF->BCF->VCF round-trip, the POS field must still refer to the position of the first base of the REF string. The relationship POS = genomic coordinate of REF[0] must be preserved. Any off-by-one error in the BCF codec would shift POS relative to REF.

evidence.quote: The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
* POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T shuffles ##meta lines (keeping ##fileformat first); the canonical JSON must be identical since meta lines other than ##fileformat may appear in any order per spec

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line field order permutation round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T reorders key=value pairs inside structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER meta-lines; implementations must not rely on field ordering within structured lines

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line shuffle preserves variant classification queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value reorder preserves variant queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT permutation preserves variant type and filter queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key=value order invariance in data lines
preconditions: at least one data record has multiple INFO sub-fields (e.g. DP=30;AF=0.5;MQ=60)
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the set of INFO key=value pairs is identical; only their semicolon-separated order differs

evidence.quote: INFO — additional information: Semicolon-separated series of additional information fields, or the MISSING value `.' if none are present.
It is recommended to respect the ordering as specified in the input VCF/BCF2 file, but parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value order is semantically meaningless, but the BCF2 records section advises parsers should not rely on ordering

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — BCF codec re-indexes dictionary entries; the order of ##contig/##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER declarations is implementation-defined and must not affect parsed output

evidence.quote: Defined this way, the dictionary of strings depends on the order and the presence of all preceding header lines.

ambiguity_flags: The BCF spec notes the dictionary depends on order, but the IDX field can be used to explicitly define positions; parsers should handle both cases

preconditions: has at least 2 sample columns in the header
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — sample columns are identified by their header ID, not column position; permuting columns preserves all sample-variant associations

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering; the MR relies on the principle that sample IDs in the header uniquely identify each sample

quarantine.oracle: After permuting sample columns uniformly across the header and all data lines, the per-sample genotype and FORMAT data must be correctly reassigned to the new column ordering. The variant semantics per sample must be preserved.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly mandate that sample column order is semantically irrelevant, but column headers and data are positionally matched, so a uniform permutation preserves semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: Trim common affixes invariance — canonical parsimonious representation
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the canonical normalized representation (parsimonious, left-anchored) must be semantically identical; any parser that normalizes must recognize both forms as the same variant

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' for complex substitutions — this MAY/SHOULD ambiguity means two parsers may disagree on whether to trim or keep the padding base, making this a valid MR to detect normalization inconsistencies

quarantine.mr_name: Left-align indel in homopolymer run invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — left-shifting an indel within a homopolymer run produces a semantically equivalent variant; a normalization-compliant parser must recognize both representations as the same variant per Tan 2015 §2.1

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec permits but does not require the padding base for indels, meaning left-alignment is not explicitly mandated — two parsers may differ on whether to left-shift, making this a valid MR to detect normalization inconsistencies

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT (with synchronized Number=A/R arrays and remapped GT indices) must represent the same set of variants; a spec-compliant parser must treat both forms as equivalent

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles. These alleles do not have to be called in any of the samples.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly mandate multi-allelic splitting as a normalization step — this is a convention from bcftools norm; parsers MAY or MAY NOT normalize multi-allelic sites, making this a valid MR to detect inconsistencies

quarantine.mr_name: Reject data line with missing mandatory fixed column (QUAL dropped)
quarantine.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a data line that has fewer than the 8 mandatory fixed columns. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The mandatory header line names the 8 fixed, mandatory columns. These columns are as follows: #CHROM POS ID REF ALT QUAL FILTER INFO

quarantine.mr_name: Telomere boundary position (0 or N+1) invariance under round-trip serialization
quarantine.oracle: Records with POS=0 or POS=N+1 (telomere positions) must survive a write-roundtrip with identical POS values. A parser that incorrectly enforces 1-based positivity (POS>=1) or clamps to [1,N] would alter these values.

evidence.quote: Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig.
If the chromosome extends from position 1 to N, then the virtual telomeric breakends are at positions 0 and N+1.

quarantine.mr_name: CIPOS confidence interval bounds relative to POS invariance under meta-line shuffling
quarantine.oracle: CIPOS confidence interval bounds are relative to POS (lower <= 0 <= upper). After shuffling meta-lines, the parsed CIPOS values and their relationship to POS must remain unchanged. The affected_start() and affected_end() query methods must return the same values.

evidence.chunk_id: VCFv4.5.tex::INFO keys used for structural variants::p607
evidence.quote: Each CIPOS interval must span 0. That is, the lower bound cannot be greater than 0, and the upper bound cannot be less than 0.
evidence.section_id: INFO keys used for structural variants

evidence.quote: The confidence interval bounds are relative to the nominal value.

ambiguity_flags: If CIPOS lower bound is '.', it is assumed to be 0; if upper bound is '.', it is assumed to be an unbounded estimate.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T permutes sample column order consistently across header and all data rows; the canonical JSON must be identical since sample identity is preserved by column headers regardless of ordering

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] All data lines are tab-delimited with no tab character at the end of the line.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that sample column order is semantically irrelevant, though the column headers identify each sample by ID, implying order is not semantically meaningful

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T shuffles semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column; the INFO field is a semicolon-separated series of key=value pairs whose order is not semantically meaningful

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] INFO --- additional information: Semicolon-separated series of additional information fields, or the MISSING value ` .' if none are present.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pair order is semantically irrelevant, though it is described as a 'series' which implies sequential parsing

quarantine.mr_name: Sample column permutation preserves per-sample query results
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that sample column order is semantically irrelevant, but sample identity is tied to column header, not position.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle preserves variant queries
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs in data lines are order-independent, but the structured line analogy suggests key-based access.

quarantine.mr_name: Missing value representation equivalence round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T appends a FORMAT field filled with '.' to all samples; a single '.' represents a missing value and is semantically a no-op per spec

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] If any of the fields is missing, it is replaced with the MISSING value. For example if the FORMAT is GT:GQ:DP:HQ then 0|0:.:23:23,34 indicates that GQ is missing.

[VCF v4.5 — Data lines] If a field contains a list of missing values, it can be represented either as a single MISSING value (`.') or as a list of missing values (e.g. `.,.,.' if the field was Number=3).
